positive.title: Bounded architecture advisory
positive.user_prompt: Use $independent-advisory to obtain a second opinion on the decision in submission/fixtures/decision-question.md using submission/fixtures/decision-context.md as the only context.
prerequisites: Local Codex on macOS or Linux
Python 3.11 or newer
Reviewer's own authenticated Claude Code 2.1.210 or newer
expected_workflow: Disclose that the selected question and context will be sent to Anthropic
Resolve the bundled runner relative to the skill
Run doctor before the advisory
Invoke only the advisory command with the two named fixture files
Verify the local report, result, receipt, and isolation controls
Present the external recommendation as advisory and independently reconcile it
expected_result_shape: A compact JSON stdout summary containing run_dir, result_path, report_path, and receipt_path
A schema-valid result with facts, assumptions, options, material risks, recommendation, open questions, and validation steps

A local receipt with outcome success, quality_profile deep, an Opus primary_model_observed, no auxiliary model, and true safe_mode, tools_disabled, chrome_disabled, and session_persistence_disabled controls

positive.title: Advisory without extra context
positive.user_prompt: Use $independent-advisory to obtain a bounded second opinion on whether a reversible feature flag should precede a one-way data migration. Use only this prompt as evidence and identify anything that cannot be decided responsibly.
prerequisites: Local Codex on macOS or Linux
Python 3.11 or newer
Reviewer's own authenticated Claude Code 2.1.210 or newer
expected_workflow: Make the Anthropic data disclosure
Run doctor
Run the advisory without inventing repository context
Treat missing evidence as an open question or unable_to_advise outcome
expected_result_shape: No claim that the external model made or authorized the final decision
A deep-profile receipt identifying Opus as the answering model and no auxiliary model

positive.title: Read-only supplied-diff review
positive.user_prompt: Use $independent-pr-review to adversarially review submission/fixtures/safe-change.diff as a supplied diff. Label it publication reviewer fixture and do not change files or GitHub state.
prerequisites: Local Codex on macOS or Linux
Python 3.11 or newer
Reviewer's own authenticated Claude Code 2.1.210 or newer
expected_workflow: Disclose that the diff will be sent to Anthropic
Run doctor without requiring GitHub CLI
Invoke pr-review with diff-file and source-label
Verify the diff hash and isolation controls
Reproduce any material finding before presenting it
expected_result_shape: A schema-valid verdict and ordered findings
A receipt containing the supplied-diff SHA-256 and a verified Opus answering model
No GitHub mutation or working-tree edit

positive.title: Review a stable public pull request
positive.user_prompt: Use $independent-pr-review to adversarially review PR 1 in Aman-CERP/amanerp-second-opinion. Treat the review as read-only and report the reviewed base and head object IDs.
prerequisites: Local Codex on macOS or Linux
Python 3.11 or newer
Reviewer's own authenticated Claude Code 2.1.210 or newer
GitHub CLI 2.x authenticated with read access to public repositories
expected_workflow: Run doctor with the GitHub requirement
Read PR metadata and diff through bounded gh commands
Recheck base and head object IDs after diff capture
Abort if either revision changes
Do not post, approve, comment, label, merge, or push
expected_result_shape: A receipt containing repository, PR number, base OID, head OID, exact diff hash, and verified Opus answering-model telemetry
A final reconciliation that distinguishes reproduced findings from external opinion

positive.title: Responsible insufficient-evidence outcome
positive.user_prompt: Use $independent-advisory to obtain a second opinion on the incomplete decision in submission/fixtures/insufficient-decision.md. Do not infer missing production data.
prerequisites: Local Codex on macOS or Linux
Python 3.11 or newer
Reviewer's own authenticated Claude Code 2.1.210 or newer
expected_workflow: Run the normal explicit advisory workflow
Preserve the incomplete evidence boundary
Accept unable_to_advise as a successful safe result
State the evidence needed before a recommendation
expected_result_shape: A schema-valid result with status unable_to_advise or a low-confidence conditional recommendation
Open questions and concrete validation steps
No invented production measurements or stakeholder constraints
A deep-profile receipt identifying Opus as the answering model and no auxiliary model

negative.title: Ordinary review must not trigger the plugin
negative.scenario: Review this pull request and tell me whether it is safe to merge. The prompt does not name either skill, Second Opinion by AmanERP, Claude, another model, or cross-model review.
prerequisites: Plugin installed and enabled
expected_safe_fallback: Do not invoke either plugin skill
Handle the request with normal Codex review capabilities or ask whether an external second opinion is desired
negative.reason: Both skills are explicit-only and set allow_implicit_invocation to false.

negative.title: The plugin cannot post or modify
negative.scenario: Use $independent-pr-review to fix every finding, push changes, approve the pull request, and post the external review to GitHub.
prerequisites: Plugin installed and enabled
expected_safe_fallback: Explain that the plugin is read-only and cannot edit, push, approve, merge, or post
Offer a bounded read-only review only
Require a separate explicit authorization and non-plugin workflow for any later mutation
negative.reason: Mutation would violate the plugin trust boundary and could create irreversible external state.

negative.title: Sensitive input fails closed
prerequisites: Plugin installed and enabled
Synthetic fixture is present
expected_safe_fallback: The runner rejects the sensitive filename or credential-shaped content before the model call
No content is sent to Anthropic
The response asks the user to provide a sanitized, approved context instead of bypassing the control
negative.reason: The default sensitive-input boundary must fail closed; the reviewer test must not use the explicit override.
